# Jira Reference — Invoca

Configuration for Jira custom fields, team mappings, and sprint operations at Invoca.

## Connection

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| Domain | `invoca.atlassian.net` |
| User email | `$(git config user.email)` |
| Keychain service | `atlassian-api-token` |

For token setup instructions, see @.agents/skills/macos-keychain/SKILL.md.

---

## Custom Field IDs

| Field | ID | Value Type | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| Story Points | `customfield_13529` | Number | `3` |
| QA Owner | `customfield_10500` | `{"accountId": "..."}` | see "Get accountId" in SKILL.md |
| Post-Deploy Checks & Monitoring | `customfield_12100` | String | `"Verify feature flag is enabled"` |

---

## Team Mapping

Maps Jira project keys to the owning team and their associated sprint board and label. Use this when:
- Assigning a ticket to a sprint (look up the team's board ID)
- Verifying a ticket has the correct team label applied
- Routing a ticket to the right reviewers

| Jira Project | Team | Sprint Board ID | Team Label                          |
|---|---|---|-------------------------------------|
| ATLAS | Frontend Platform | 218 | `frontend-platform-team`            |
| BACON | Bacon | 612 | _(unknown — check live)_            |
| BARN | Customer Data (Barnacles) | 163 | `customer-data-team`                |
| BIRDS | Birds | 448 | `integrations-team`                 |
| IONIC | Prod Support | 170 | `ps` _(may be incomplete)_          |
| IRIS | Conversational Messaging | 348 | `conversational-messaging`          |
| JETS | JETS | 216 | `contact-center-integrations-team`  |
| MON | Monarch | 513 | `conversation-intelligence-monarch` |
| OCTO | Octothorpe | 217 | `octothorpe`                        |
| ODYS | Odyssey Data Platform | 415 | `data_platform`                     |
| OMEGA | Omega Backend Reporting | 645 | `reporting-backend-team`            |
| PHX | Phoenix (Calls) | 161 | `calls-team`                        |
| RANG | Rangers | 546 | `campaigns-attribution-team`        |
| SRE | SRE Core | 447 | _(unknown — check live)_            |
| TELNET | Telnet | 678 | _(unknown — check live)_            |
| THUND | AI Platform | 249 | `ai-platform-team`                  |
| TRI | Trident Reporting Frontend | 646 | `reporting-frontend-team`           |

> **Note**: This mapping was last verified in March 2026 and may be out of date. Teams are reorganized periodically, so confirm with the user before relying on it — especially for sprint assignment, label application, or routing. Labels marked "unknown" had no consistent label on sampled tickets and require live verification: `acli jira workitem search --jql "project = <KEY> AND labels is not EMPTY" --fields labels`

### How to look up a board ID

```bash
# List all Scrum boards
acli jira board search --type scrum --paginate --json
```

### Sprint assignment

```bash
# List active/future sprints for a board
acli jira board get-sprints --id <BOARD_ID> --state active,future

# Assign issue to a sprint
JIRA_USER=$(git config user.email)
JIRA_TOKEN=$(security find-generic-password -s "atlassian-api-token" -a "${JIRA_USER}" -w)
JIRA_AUTH=$(echo -n "${JIRA_USER}:${JIRA_TOKEN}" | base64)

curl -s -X POST "https://invoca.atlassian.net/rest/agile/1.0/sprint/${SPRINT_ID}/issue" \
  -H "Authorization: Basic ${JIRA_AUTH}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"issues\": [\"${TICKET_ID}\"]}"
```

### Team label

Fetch existing labels first to avoid overwriting them, then add the team label:

```bash
# Fetch current labels
CURRENT_LABELS=$(curl -s "https://invoca.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/issue/${TICKET_ID}?fields=labels" \
  -H "Authorization: Basic ${JIRA_AUTH}" \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; labels=json.load(sys.stdin)['fields']['labels']; print(json.dumps([l['name'] for l in labels]))")

# Append team label and PUT back
python3 - <<'EOF'
import subprocess, json, os

ticket_id = os.environ['TICKET_ID']
jira_auth = os.environ['JIRA_AUTH']
current_labels = json.loads(os.environ['CURRENT_LABELS'])
team_label = "<team-label>"

if team_label not in current_labels:
    current_labels.append(team_label)

body = json.dumps({"fields": {"labels": current_labels}})
subprocess.run([
    "curl", "-s", "-X", "PUT",
    f"https://invoca.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/issue/{ticket_id}",
    "-H", f"Authorization: Basic {jira_auth}",
    "-H", "Content-Type: application/json",
    "-d", body
])
EOF
```
